2025 Occupation Shortage List (OSL)

The Occupation Shortage List (OSL), compiled by Jobs and Skills Australia , identifies occupations experiencing shortages across the country and within each state and territory. It includes 916 occupations drawn from ANZSCO version 2022 and covers skill levels 1 to 4, which are most closely linked to post-compulsory education and training pathways. The OSL provides a snapshot of labour-market conditions and serves as an evidence base for assessing occupational demand across Australia.

Occupation Ratings

  • S Shortage: Employers struggle to fill vacancies for a particular occupation due to skill gaps, pay levels, or job conditions, even in accessible locations. Sometimes shortages only affect specific specializations within an occupation.
  • M Metropolitan Shortage: Shortages are limited to metropolitan areas.
  • R Regional Shortage: Shortages are restricted to regional areas.
  • NS No Shortage: No significant hiring challenges have been identified, or there is insufficient evidence of a shortage.
